"'''The Paradise Detour'''" is a [[Star Wars (comic strip)|comic strip]] written by [[Archie Goodwin]] and illustrated by [[Al Williamson]]. It was published by the [[Los Angeles Times Syndicate]] in [[1983]]. It was collected in the trade paperback, ''[[Classic Star Wars Volume 3: Escape to Hoth]]''.

==Plot summary==
The [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']] experienced [[Navigation computer/Legends|navigation computer]] problems and landed on a planet for repairs. While [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] tried to fix the ship, [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] went off to explore.

He saw a woman from his past, [[Tanith Shire]], caught by some sort of monstrous plant. He freed her with his [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]], but she ran off into the forest and he gave chase. When he caught her, Luke saw she was not really Tanith Shire. They both fell into the river. When they got out, she told him her name was [[S'ybll]], and that the air of her planet could be intoxicating. As they walked, Luke saw [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtrooper]] armor. He pulled out his lightsaber to slash it, revealing an empty suit. S'ybll explained she used it to scare off attackers, as this could be a dangerous planet. Seeing a damaged [[Lambda-class T-4a shuttle/Legends|''Lambda''-class shuttle]], Luke commented on how odd it was to have this near one's home, but S'ybll testily replied it had been there before her. Suddenly, they were attacked by something that looked like the [[Night Beast]], which had previously plagued the Rebels. Running away, Luke slipped and fell. Then his mentor, [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]], appeared to him and told him he must go protect his new companion, S'ybll. Luke then fainted.

When he woke, there was a cloth over his eyes. S'ybll told him not to remove it, but he stood up upon hearing [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] trying to contact him on his [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]], which he thought he'd lost in the river. He thus saw S'ybll's true form, that of an old woman. She told him he was now under her spell. He tried to escape from her, but she entered his mind to drain his life force. She was a [[mind-witch]], and had been casting illusions on him as distractions. She projected an illusion of [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]]. Luke realized that he must remain calm, and the illusion disappeared as Vader's phantom lightsaber passed through him. Angered, S'ybll tried to kill him by [[Telekinesis/Legends|throwing]] a tree at him. Luke dodged, so S'ybll tried to hit him from multiple directions. Luke ran towards her, and her final attempt ended up killing her as she drained the last of her power. Luke rejoined his friends and they continued their journey to [[Echo Base/Legends|Echo Base]].

==Continuity==
''The Paradise Detour'' is the 13th comic strip of the [[Star Wars (comic strip)|''Star Wars'' comic series]] written by [[Archie Goodwin]] and illustrated by [[Al Williamson]] in the early 1980s (and later reprinted in [[Classic Star Wars 1|''Classic Star Wars'' 1]]-[[Classic Star Wars 20|20]] in the 1990s).

The series was written in a manner showing the stories unfold one after the other without interruption.<ref name="TCC">''[[Star Wars: The Comics Companion]]''</ref> The novel ''[[A New Hope: The Life of Luke Skywalker]]'', which reused content from the comic strips, also presented a direct continuity between the stories.<ref name="ANHTLoLS">''[[A New Hope: The Life of Luke Skywalker]]''</ref> However, [[Lucasfilm]] contested this chronology because the existence of [[Echo Base/Legends|Echo Base]] at the time of the [[Evacuation of Yavin]] (in [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]]) was in contradiction with other ''Star Wars'' sources. Lucasfilm therefore introduced a gap of several years between "[[Race for Survival]]" and "The Paradise Detour" (between [[Classic Star Wars 16|''Classic Star Wars'' 16]] and [[Classic Star Wars 17|17]]).<ref name="TCC" /> Accordingly, [[The Essential Chronology (real-world book)|''The Essential Chronology'']] dated ''Classic Star Wars'' 17-20 to [[2 ABY/Legends|2]]-[[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]].<ref name="TEC" />
